@@ -0,0 +1,408 @@
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Catalog utilities — display labels, filters, sorting, product helpers.
+// Types are inlined here to keep lib/ independent of src/.
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export const CATALOG_QUERY_KEY = ["catalog"] as const;
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Types
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export type ServiceTypeId =
+  | "advisory"
+  | "design"
+  | "ai_design"
+  | "deploy"
+  | "ai_deploy"
+  | "manage"
+  | "bundle";
+
+export type DeliveryComplexity = "low" | "medium" | "high";
+
+export interface ServiceProduct {
+  id: number;
+  collection: string;
+  serviceType: ServiceTypeId;
+  standardName: string;
+  remixName: Record<string, string>;
+  description: string;
+  positioning: string;
+  price: string;
+  duration: string;
+  popularityRank: number;
+  deliveryComplexity: DeliveryComplexity;
+  badge: string;
+  audience: string;
+  industryRelevance: string;
+  features: string[];
+  tags: string[];
+  outcomes: string[];
+  department: string;
+  businessImpact: string;
+  implementationModel: string;
+  timelineMilestones: string[];
+  relatedServices: number[];
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// serviceProductUtils
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export function getRemixedName(
+  service: ServiceProduct,
+  remixKey: string = "all"
+): string {
+  if (remixKey === "all") return service.standardName;
+  return service.remixName[remixKey] ?? service.standardName;
+}
+
+const PRICE_DISPLAY_OVERRIDES: Record<string, string> = {
+  Custom: "Pricing on request",
+  "Custom Monthly Plans": "Pricing on request",
+};
+
+export function formatPriceDisplay(price: string): string {
+  const trimmed = price.trim();
+  return PRICE_DISPLAY_OVERRIDES[trimmed] ?? trimmed;
+}
+
+export function parseServicePrice(price: string): number {
+  return Number(price.replace(/[^0-9]/g, "")) || 0;
+}
+
+export function formatServicePrice(amount: number): string {
+  return `$${amount.toLocaleString("en-US")}`;
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// marketplaceDisplayLabels
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+const VARIANT_SUFFIX_RE =
+  /^(.+?)\s+-\s+(Assess|Design|AI Design|Deploy|AI Deploy|Managed|Managed Service|Transformation Bundle|Advisory Set|Design Services Set|Deploy Services Set|Managed Services Set)$/;
+
+export const PRODUCT_DISPLAY_RENAMES: Record<string, string> = {
+  "Mobile Apps": "Mobile Apps & Services",
+  "Physical Channels": "Physical & Frontline Channels",
+  "CRM Solutions": "CRM & Customer Relationship",
+  "Marketing Solutions": "Marketing Operations",
+  "Advisory Set": "Flexible Advisory Package",
+  "Design Services Set": "Flexible Design Package",
+  "Deploy Services Set": "Flexible Deploy Package",
+  "Managed Services Set": "Flexible Managed Services Package",
+};
+
+const SUFFIX_TO_SERVICE_TYPE: Record<string, string> = {
+  Assess: "advisory",
+  Design: "design",
+  "AI Design": "ai_design",
+  Deploy: "deploy",
+  "AI Deploy": "ai_deploy",
+  Managed: "manage",
+  "Managed Service": "manage",
+  "Transformation Bundle": "bundle",
+  "Advisory Set": "bundle",
+  "Design Services Set": "bundle",
+  "Deploy Services Set": "bundle",
+  "Managed Services Set": "bundle",
+};
+
+const CARD_STAGE_LABEL: Record<string, string> = {
+  advisory: "Assess",
+  design: "Design",
+  ai_design: "AI Design",
+  deploy: "Deploy",
+  ai_deploy: "AI Deploy",
+  manage: "Managed Service",
+  bundle: "End-to-end bundle",
+};
+
+const PDP_TYPE_BADGE_LABEL: Record<string, string> = {
+  advisory: "Assessment",
+  design: "Design",
+  ai_design: "AI Design",
+  deploy: "Deploy",
+  ai_deploy: "AI Deploy",
+  manage: "Managed Service",
+  bundle: "End-to-end bundle",
+};
+
+export const MARKETPLACE_SERVICE_TYPE_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {
+  advisory: "Assess",
+  design: "Design",
+  ai_design: "AI Design",
+  deploy: "Deploy",
+  ai_deploy: "AI Deploy",
+  manage: "Managed Service",
+  bundle: "End-to-end bundle",
+};
+
+export const BUNDLE_PDP_SUBTITLE =
+  "Full transformation path — assessment, design, deployment, and managed service, scoped to your priorities.";
+
+export const BUNDLE_PDP_SUBTITLE_SHORT =
+  "Advisory through managed operations, tailored to your organisation.";
+
+export const BUNDLE_INCLUSION_LINE =
+  "Includes design, AI design, deploy, AI deploy, and managed service stages, scoped during assessment.";
+
+type ParsedName = {
+  productStem: string;
+  variantSuffix: string | null;
+  serviceType: string | undefined;
+};
+
+function parseStandardName(standardName: string): ParsedName {
+  const withoutHighImpact = standardName.replace(" (High-Impact)", "").trim();
+  const match = withoutHighImpact.match(VARIANT_SUFFIX_RE);
+  if (!match) {
+    return { productStem: withoutHighImpact, variantSuffix: null, serviceType: undefined };
+  }
+  const variantSuffix = match[2]!;
+  return {
+    productStem: match[1]!.trim(),
+    variantSuffix,
+    serviceType: SUFFIX_TO_SERVICE_TYPE[variantSuffix],
+  };
+}
+
+function renameProductStem(stem: string): string {
+  return PRODUCT_DISPLAY_RENAMES[stem] ?? stem;
+}
+
+function resolveServiceType(
+  standardName: string,
+  serviceType?: string
+): string | undefined {
+  if (serviceType) return serviceType;
+  return parseStandardName(standardName).serviceType;
+}
+
+const PDP_BUNDLE_TITLE_STAGE = "End-to-end transformation";
+
+function formatTitle(
+  standardName: string,
+  serviceType: string | undefined,
+  stageLabel: string | undefined,
+  bundleTitleStage: string = PDP_BUNDLE_TITLE_STAGE
+): string {
+  const { productStem, variantSuffix } = parseStandardName(standardName);
+  const product = renameProductStem(productStem);
+
+  if (variantSuffix && productStem === variantSuffix) {
+    return product;
+  }
+
+  if (serviceType === "bundle" || variantSuffix === "Transformation Bundle") {
+    return `${product} - ${bundleTitleStage}`;
+  }
+
+  const stage =
+    stageLabel ??
+    (serviceType ? CARD_STAGE_LABEL[serviceType] : variantSuffix) ??
+    variantSuffix;
+
+  if (!stage) return product;
+  return `${product} - ${stage}`;
+}
+
+export function getMarketplaceCardTitle(
+  standardName: string,
+  serviceType?: string
+): string {
+  const type = resolveServiceType(standardName, serviceType);
+  const stage = type ? CARD_STAGE_LABEL[type] : undefined;
+  return formatTitle(standardName, type, stage, CARD_STAGE_LABEL["bundle"]);
+}
+
+export function getPdpDisplayTitle(
+  standardName: string,
+  serviceType?: string
+): string {
+  const type = resolveServiceType(standardName, serviceType);
+  const stage = type ? CARD_STAGE_LABEL[type] : undefined;
+  return formatTitle(standardName, type, stage, PDP_BUNDLE_TITLE_STAGE);
+}
+
+/** @deprecated Use getPdpDisplayTitle or getMarketplaceCardTitle */
+export function getDisplayTitle(
+  standardName: string,
+  serviceType?: string
+): string {
+  return getPdpDisplayTitle(standardName, serviceType);
+}
+
+export function getPdpTypeBadgeLabel(serviceType: string, badge?: string): string {
+  if (serviceType === "bundle") return PDP_TYPE_BADGE_LABEL["bundle"]!;
+  return PDP_TYPE_BADGE_LABEL[serviceType] ?? badge ?? serviceType;
+}
+
+export function getDisplayProductName(standardName: string): string {
+  const { productStem } = parseStandardName(standardName);
+  return renameProductStem(productStem);
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// marketplaceCatalogFilters
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export type MarketplaceSortBy = "popular" | "price-low" | "fastest";
+
+const SERVICE_TYPE_INTERLEAVE_ORDER: ServiceTypeId[] = [
+  "advisory",
+  "design",
+  "ai_design",
+  "deploy",
+  "ai_deploy",
+  "manage",
+  "bundle",
+];
+
+function compareWithinPopularityBucket(
+  a: ServiceProduct,
+  b: ServiceProduct
+): number {
+  return b.popularityRank - a.popularityRank || a.id - b.id;
+}
+
+export function sortCatalogByPopularMix(
+  services: ServiceProduct[]
+): ServiceProduct[] {
+  const buckets = new Map<ServiceTypeId, ServiceProduct[]>();
+
+  for (const type of SERVICE_TYPE_INTERLEAVE_ORDER) {
+    buckets.set(type, []);
+  }
+
+  for (const service of services) {
+    const bucket = buckets.get(service.serviceType);
+    if (bucket) {
+      bucket.push(service);
+    } else {
+      buckets.set(service.serviceType, [service]);
+    }
+  }
+
+  for (const bucket of buckets.values()) {
+    bucket.sort(compareWithinPopularityBucket);
+  }
+
+  const activeTypes = SERVICE_TYPE_INTERLEAVE_ORDER.filter(
+    (type) => (buckets.get(type)?.length ?? 0) > 0
+  );
+
+  const mixed: ServiceProduct[] = [];
+  while (true) {
+    let added = 0;
+    for (const type of activeTypes) {
+      const bucket = buckets.get(type);
+      const next = bucket?.shift();
+      if (next) {
+        mixed.push(next);
+        added++;
+      }
+    }
+    if (added === 0) break;
+  }
+
+  return mixed;
+}
+
+export type MarketplaceCatalogFilters = {
+  activeTab: string;
+  searchQuery: string;
+  selectedCategories: string[];
+  selectedServiceTypes: string[];
+  selectedIncluded: string[];
+  selectedSectors: string[];
+  sortBy: MarketplaceSortBy;
+  excludeVariantIds?: number[];
+};
+
+function compareServices(
+  a: ServiceProduct,
+  b: ServiceProduct,
+  sortBy: MarketplaceSortBy
+): number {
+  if (sortBy === "popular") return b.popularityRank - a.popularityRank;
+  if (sortBy === "price-low") {
+    return (
+      parseInt(a.price.replace(/[$,]/g, ""), 10) -
+      parseInt(b.price.replace(/[$,]/g, ""), 10)
+    );
+  }
+  if (sortBy === "fastest") {
+    return parseInt(a.duration, 10) - parseInt(b.duration, 10);
+  }
+  return 0;
+}
+
+export function filterCatalogServices(
+  catalog: ServiceProduct[],
+  filters: MarketplaceCatalogFilters
+): ServiceProduct[] {
+  const {
+    activeTab,
+    searchQuery,
+    selectedCategories,
+    selectedServiceTypes,
+    selectedIncluded,
+    selectedSectors,
+    sortBy,
+    excludeVariantIds = [],
+  } = filters;
+
+  const excludeIds = new Set(excludeVariantIds);
+  const wantsMultiOnly =
+    selectedIncluded.includes("multi") && !selectedIncluded.includes("single");
+  const wantsSingleOnly =
+    selectedIncluded.includes("single") && !selectedIncluded.includes("multi");
+  const activeSector = selectedSectors.length > 0 ? selectedSectors[0]! : "all";
+
+  const filtered = catalog.filter((pkg) => {
+    if (excludeIds.has(pkg.id)) return false;
+
+    const isBundle = pkg.serviceType === "bundle";
+
+    if (activeTab === "bundles") {
+      if (!isBundle) return false;
+    } else if (wantsMultiOnly) {
+      if (!isBundle) return false;
+    } else if (wantsSingleOnly) {
+      if (isBundle) return false;
+    } else if (isBundle) {
+      return false;
+    }
+
+    const matchesCollection =
+      activeTab === "all" || activeTab === "bundles" || pkg.collection === activeTab;
+
+    const matchesCategory =
+      selectedCategories.length === 0 || selectedCategories.includes(pkg.collection);
+
+    const matchesServiceType =
+      selectedServiceTypes.length === 0 ||
+      selectedServiceTypes.includes(pkg.serviceType);
+
+    const matchesSearch =
+      searchQuery === "" ||
+      pkg.standardName.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) ||
+      pkg.description.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) ||
+      getRemixedName(pkg, activeSector)
+        .toLowerCase()
+        .includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) ||
+      pkg.tags.some((tag) => tag.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())) ||
+      pkg.features.some((feat) =>
+        feat.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())
+      );
+
+    return matchesCollection && matchesCategory && matchesServiceType && matchesSearch;
+  });
+
+  if (sortBy === "popular") {
+    return sortCatalogByPopularMix(filtered);
+  }
+
+  return filtered.sort((a, b) => compareServices(a, b, sortBy));
+}

+import type { ServiceProduct } from "@/lib/types/serviceProduct";
 
 const CACHE_KEY = "tmaas-marketplace-catalog-v2";
 const CACHE_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;

diff --git a/lib/config/index.ts b/lib/config/index.ts
new file mode 100644

@@ -0,0 +1,123 @@
+export const config = {
+  supabaseUrl: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL ?? "",
+  supabaseAnonKey: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY ?? "",
+  appName: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME ?? "TMaaS",
+  env: (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV ?? "development") as
+    | "development"
+    | "staging"
+    | "production",
+} as const;
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Feature flags
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export interface FeatureFlags {
+  // MVP pages
+  homepage: boolean;
+  marketplace: boolean;
+  serviceDetail: boolean;
+  contactUs: boolean;
+
+  // Additional pages
+  explore: boolean;
+  alternateLanding: boolean;
+  landingV1: boolean;
+  legal: boolean;
+
+  // Features
+  supabaseCatalog: boolean;
+  cart: boolean;
+  chatAssistant: boolean;
+  auth: boolean;
+  onboarding: boolean;
+  dashboard: boolean;
+  butlerDemo: boolean;
+  contextSwitcher: boolean;
+}
+
+const PAGE_FLAGS: (keyof FeatureFlags)[] = [
+  "homepage",
+  "marketplace",
+  "serviceDetail",
+  "contactUs",
+  "explore",
+  "alternateLanding",
+  "landingV1",
+  "legal",
+];
+
+const ROUTE_PRIORITY: { flag: keyof FeatureFlags; path: string }[] = [
+  { flag: "homepage", path: "/" },
+  { flag: "marketplace", path: "/marketplace" },
+  { flag: "serviceDetail", path: "/marketplace" },
+  { flag: "contactUs", path: "/contact" },
+];
+
+const DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS: FeatureFlags = {
+  homepage: true,
+  marketplace: true,
+  serviceDetail: true,
+  contactUs: true,
+
+  explore: false,
+  alternateLanding: false,
+  landingV1: false,
+  legal: true,
+
+  supabaseCatalog: true,
+  cart: false,
+  chatAssistant: false,
+  auth: false,
+  onboarding: false,
+  dashboard: false,
+  butlerDemo: false,
+  contextSwitcher: false,
+};
+
+class FeatureFlagService {
+  private flags: FeatureFlags = { ...DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS };
+
+  getFlags(): FeatureFlags {
+    return { ...this.flags };
+  }
+
+  isEnabled(feature: keyof FeatureFlags): boolean {
+    return this.flags[feature] ?? false;
+  }
+
+  enable(feature: keyof FeatureFlags): void {
+    this.flags[feature] = true;
+  }
+
+  disable(feature: keyof FeatureFlags): void {
+    this.flags[feature] = false;
+  }
+
+  setFlags(flags: Partial<FeatureFlags>): void {
+    this.flags = { ...this.flags, ...flags };
+  }
+
+  resetToDefaults(): void {
+    this.flags = { ...DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS };
+  }
+}
+
+export const featureFlags = new FeatureFlagService();
+
+export const isPageEnabled = (page: keyof FeatureFlags) =>
+  featureFlags.isEnabled(page);
+export const isFeatureEnabled = (feature: keyof FeatureFlags) =>
+  featureFlags.isEnabled(feature);
+
+export const getEnabledPages = () => {
+  const flags = featureFlags.getFlags();
+  return PAGE_FLAGS.filter((key) => flags[key]);
+};
+
+export const getFirstEnabledRoute = (): string => {
+  for (const { flag, path } of ROUTE_PRIORITY) {
+    if (featureFlags.isEnabled(flag)) return path;

+  return "/";

+export const logger = {
+  info: (msg: string, ctx?: Record<string, unknown>) =>
+    console.log(JSON.stringify({ level: "info", msg, ...ctx })),
+  warn: (msg: string, ctx?: Record<string, unknown>) =>
+    console.warn(JSON.stringify({ level: "warn", msg, ...ctx })),
+  error: (msg: string, ctx?: Record<string, unknown>) =>
+    console.error(JSON.stringify({ level: "error", msg, ...ctx })),
+};

+export function truncateMetaDescription(text: string, maxLength = 155): string {
+  const trimmed = text.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
+  if (trimmed.length <= maxLength) return trimmed;
+  const slice = trimmed.slice(0, maxLength - 1);
+  const lastSpace = slice.lastIndexOf(" ");
+  return `${(lastSpace > 80 ? slice.slice(0, lastSpace) : slice).trim()}…`;
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
+import { createClient, type SupabaseClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";
+
+const supabaseUrl =
+  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL ??
+  process.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL;
+const supabaseAnonKey =
+  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY ??
+  process.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY;
+
+export const isSupabaseConfigured = (): boolean =>
+  Boolean(supabaseUrl && supabaseAnonKey);
+
+// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
+let client: SupabaseClient<any> | null = null;
+
+// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
+export const getSupabaseClient = (): SupabaseClient<any> | null => {
+  if (!isSupabaseConfigured()) return null;
+  if (!client) {
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
+    client = createClient<any>(supabaseUrl!, supabaseAnonKey!);
+  }
+  return client;
+};

diff --git a/specs/26.05_project-delivery_specs_v0.5.md b/specs/26.05_project-delivery_specs_v0.5.md
deleted file mode 100644
index f410a8f..0000000
--- a/specs/26.05_project-delivery_specs_v0.5.md

@@ -1,260 +0,0 @@
-# Specification: Project Delivery (Engagements) — DQ Delivery Lead (Stage 03)
-
----
-
-## Story: The Optimised Delivery Governance Model
-
-As a Delivery Lead (DQ), I want to manage project engagements through an objective, architecture-led governance model, so that I can eliminate ambiguity around successful delivery, automatically calculate delivery health, link operational milestones to strategic outcomes, and maintain actionable visibility of blockers and escalations.
-
-### The Governance Backbone (Three-Layer Architecture)
-
-To move away from static, subjective project states (Active / Paused / Completed), the platform models a formal relationship between three distinct layers:
-
-1. **Project Vision & KRIs (Executive Governance Layer)**
-   - Defines the internal project vision and measurable **Key Result Indicators (KRIs)** that serve as objective evidence of value realization.
-   - KRIs define what "successful delivery" means (e.g., *“A deployment is only considered successful if all department users actively utilize the platform within 90 days post go-live”*).
-   - This layer removes all ambiguity around "successful delivery" and executive reporting.
-
-2. **Milestones & Deliverables (Operational Governance Layer)**
-   - Outlines the milestones and deliverables that represent operational progress.
-   - **Milestone-to-Outcome Linkage**: Every milestone must directly map back to at least one strategic outcome/KRI.
-   - **Deliverable nesting**: Every deliverable resides under an explicit milestone, showing exactly how daily progress maps to executive goals.
-
-3. **Tasks (Execution Layer)**
-   - The execution tasks themselves are managed in external project management tools, but their metadata is formally modeled within the platform for complete governance traceability.
-   - For every Deliverable, the platform tracks:
-     - The list of associated tasks
-     - Relevant task status
-     - Percentage progress
-     - Owners
-     - Deep-links to each task in the external execution tool.
-   - This mapping provides end-to-end traceability from a developer's ticket up to the executive project vision.
-
----
-
-## Purpose (Why):
-
-TMaaS project delivery requires a shift from passive tracking to proactive, objective governance:
-- **Objective Health Logic**: Eliminate "watermelon projects" (green on the outside, red on the inside) by basing status on hard criteria rather than subjective self-reporting.
-- **Automated Status Calculation**: Dynamically compute status from underlying operational and contractual facts.
-- **Governance Traceability**: Establish a clear thread from individual tasks to deliverables, deliverables to milestones, and milestones to business outcomes.
-- **Actionable Escalation Visibility**: Give stakeholders immediate, front-and-center visibility into why a project is off-track, what is blocked, and what actions are planned to resolve it.
-
----
-
-## Offering (What):
-
-The Project Delivery feature provides:
-- A **listings screen** showing all active engagements with computed health indicators.
-- A **project details workspace** featuring a persistent header showing the automated RAG status across 7 key indicators.
-- A **tabbed workspace** structured to reflect the optimized delivery hierarchy:
-  1. **Overview Dashboard** (operational control center)
-  2. **Delivery** (Milestones & Deliverables mapped to outcomes)
-  3. **Governance** (RAID & Stakeholders)
-  4. **Commercials** (Contract, Payments & Changes)
-  5. **Sessions** (Collaboration governance)
-  6. **Messages** (Communication)
-  7. **Team** (Project roster)
-
----
-
-## 1. Engagements Listing Screen
-
-The starting point for portfolio governance. Displays all project engagements with automated health visibility.
-
-### 1.1 Page Header
-- Title: **Active Engagements**
-- Subtitle: "Manage portfolio delivery and governance"
-- **Refresh** button: Always visible
-- **Add Project** button: Opens the Add Project modal
-
-### 1.2 Metrics Cards
-A dashboard summary showing delivery distribution:
-- **Total**: Total count of all engagements.
-- **On Track (Green)**: Count of healthy engagements.
-- **At Risk (Amber)**: Count of engagements with warning indicators.
-- **Critical (Red)**: Count of engagements with active blockers or overdue milestones.
-- **Completed**: Closed engagements.
-
-### 1.3 Filters & Search
-- **Search**: Searches across project name, organization, and contact.
-- **Health Status**: All / Green / Amber / Red.
-- **Category**: Service category dropdown.
-- **Sort**: Newest First / Oldest First / Health (Red first).
-- **Date Range**: Start Date + End Date.
-- **Export**: Exports the listing with health and KRI metrics.
-- **Clear Filters**: Resets all filters to default.
-
-### 1.4 Projects Table
-- **Project Name**: Click to navigate to details.
-- **Organisation**: Client organization name.
-- **Overall Health**: Aggregated RAG status based on the 7 indicators.
-- **KRIs at Risk**: Count of executive indicators currently flagging warnings.
-- **Blocked Items**: Count of currently blocked tasks/RAID items.
-- **Lead**: Assigned delivery lead (assigned/unassigned indicator).
-- **Actions**: Assign Lead, Delete.
-
----
-
-## 2. Engagement Header & Automated Project Status
-
-Every project detail page includes a persistent, context-retaining header.
-
-### 2.1 Header Components
-- Client organisation logo or avatar.
-- Project name (display only).
-- Service Type (inline editable: Design / Deploy / Design & Deploy).
-- Project Status Indicators: Displays a visual RAG status bar showing the 7 indicators. Clicking this opens the detailed **Automated Project Status Modal**.
-
-### 2.2 Automated Project Status (The 7 Key Indicators)
-Status is never set manually. The platform calculates each indicator using objective criteria:
-
-| Indicator | Metric / Logic | Calculated RAG State |
-|---|---|---|
-| **a. Progress vs Schedule** | Based on milestone and deliverable timelines. | **Green**: 0 overdue milestones or deliverables.<br>**Amber**: Overdue deliverables, but no overdue milestones.<br>**Red**: One or more milestones are overdue past their set due dates. |
-| **b. Scope Confirmed** | Checks if all scope is defined and stable. | **Green**: All deliverables defined, and 0 deliverables associated with a Contract Change that is in a 'New', 'In Negotiation', or 'In Specification' status.<br>**Amber**: Active contract changes exist but are in advanced review.<br>**Red**: Key deliverables are missing definition OR contract changes are pending specification. |
-| **c. Plan Confirmed** | Checks complete planning readiness. | **Green**: All milestones have due dates set AND every deliverable and task has a designated owner.<br>**Amber**: Milestone due dates set, but some deliverables/tasks lack assigned owners.<br>**Red**: Milestones exist without set due dates. |
-| **d. Dependencies Met** | Identifies blocking issues. | **Green**: 0 blocked tasks AND all dependency (RAID) items are closed.<br>**Amber**: No tasks blocked, but open dependencies exist (not yet overdue).<br>**Red**: At least one task is blocked OR a dependency RAID item is past its due date. |
-| **e. Contractual Status** | Validates milestone formalization. | **Green**: All milestones have their contractual status marked as "In Place".<br>**Amber**: Milestones are in "Pending" or "In Negotiation" status.<br>**Red**: No milestones are defined OR one or more milestones are marked as "Not Started". |
-| **f. Stakeholders Committed** | Measures client commitment and updates. | **Green**: Stakeholder catalogue updated within the last 7 days AND all priority stakeholders are marked as actively engaged.<br>**Amber**: Priority stakeholders engaged, but catalogue has not been updated in >7 days.<br>**Red**: Priority stakeholders are marked as unengaged or resistant. |
-| **g. Business Benefits** | Evaluates post-go-live outcome realization. | **Green**: Project KPIs and KRIs are defined and progressing/realizing value as expected.<br>**Amber**: KRIs defined but currently showing values below the target threshold.<br>**Red**: KRIs are not yet defined OR have failed to hit target thresholds post go-live. |
-
----
-
-## 3. Tab Structure (Engagement Workspace)
-
----
-
-### 3.1 Overview Dashboard (Operational Control Center)
-
-A high-density dashboard that answers operational questions immediately.
-
-#### Immediate Answers Block:
-- **Why is the project red?**
-  - Synthesized summary highlighting the failing indicators from the 7 key health rules.
-- **What is blocked?**
-  - List of blocked tasks and unresolved dependency RAID items.
-- **What is overdue?**
-  - List of milestones and deliverables that have passed their target dates without completion.
-- **Which KRIs are at risk?**
-  - Highlights specific Key Result Indicators failing to meet target realization thresholds.
-- **What are the highest-impact escalations?**
-  - Surfaces active issues flagged as High Severity or marked for escalation in the RAID register.
-
-#### Weekly Governance Focus:
-A curated widget dedicated to weekly status reviews, restricting the view only to:
-- **High-Impact RAID items** (High severity Risks/Issues)
-- **Active Blockers**
-- **Action Items planned for the current week**
-- **Milestone Activities** in progress or scheduled for the next 7 days
-
----
-
-### 3.2 Delivery
-
-This tab manages the core execution and maps it directly to strategic goals.
-
-#### A. Project Vision & KRIs
-- **Vision Statement**: DQ's internal strategic vision for the engagement.
-- **Measurable KRIs**:
-  - List of Key Result Indicators.
-  - **Outcome Evidence**: Stored metric or evidence proof (e.g., document link, analytics query result) showing value realization.
-  - **Status**: Visual indicator showing if the KRI is on track.
-
-#### B. Milestones
-- List of milestones representing the operational schedule.
-- **Outcome Mapping**: Every milestone displays a list of the specific KRIs/outcomes it directly supports.
-- **Contractual Status**: Not Started / Pending / In Negotiation / In Place.
-- **Timeline**: Start date, target due date, and actual completion date.
-
-#### C. Deliverables & Tasks
-- Lists all deliverables nested under their respective milestone.
-- **Deliverable details**: Description, owner, status.
-- **Execution Task Board (The Task Modeling Layer)**:
-  - Formally models external tasks for each deliverable:
-    - **Task Name**
-    - **Status** (To Do / In Progress / Blocked / Done)
-    - **% Progress**
-    - **Owner**
-    - **External Link**: Deep-link to the task in JIRA, Linear, or other external execution tools.
-
----
-
-### 3.3 Governance
-
-Consolidates RAID management and stakeholder tracking.
-
-#### A. RAID Register
-Tracks Risks, Issues, Dependencies, and Assumptions (RAID).
-- **Sub-tabs**: Risks, Issues, Dependencies, Assumptions (each with a count badge).
-- **Properties**: Title, description, severity, status, owner, due date, mitigation notes, and an **Escalated** flag.
-- **Escalation Visibility**: Setting the Escalated flag automatically bubbles the item up to the Overview Dashboard.
-
-#### B. Stakeholder Catalogue
-- Registry of key client and internal stakeholders.
-- **Role/Influence**: Map stakeholder influence (High/Medium/Low) and commitment level.
-- **Engagement History**: Track contact logs and regular touchpoints.
-- **Catalogue Activity Log**: Shows the timestamp of the last catalogue update (used to drive the "Stakeholders Committed" green health rule).
-
----
-
-### 3.4 Commercials
-
-Manages the financial and legal health of the project, feeding the automated status.
-
-- **Contract Status**: Total contract value, currency, and general commercial terms.
-- **Payment Milestones + Status**:
-  - Maps milestones to payment amounts and statuses (Pending / Invoiced / Partially Paid / Paid).
-- **Commercial Documents**: Repository for signed statements of work, addenda, and terms.
-- **Contract Changes**:
-  - Track change orders (New, In Negotiation, In Specification, Approved).
-  - Shows value changes, timeline impacts, and links to affected deliverables (for automated "Scope Confirmed" calculation).
-
----
-
-### 3.5 Sessions
-- **Upcoming & Past Sessions**: Structure meetings, steering committees, and reviews.
-- **Action items**: Record actions, owners, and due dates directly generated during sessions (linked to Weekly Governance).
-
----
-
-### 3.6 Messages
-- Channels for messaging and exchanging documentation with active client contacts.
-
----
-
-### 3.7 Team
-- Roster of delivery professionals assigned to the engagement (e.g., Delivery Lead, Business Analyst, Architect), managing assignment fields to drive the "Plan Confirmed" metric.
-
----
-
-## 4. Roles & Responsibilities
-
-### Delivery Lead (DQ)
-- Owns the updates of the Stakeholder Catalogue, RAID details, and Milestone-to-Outcome mapping.
-- Oversees the mapping of external tasks to deliverables.
-- Reviews automated status calculations to coordinate recovery plans.
-- Drives sessions, mitigates dependencies, and registers contract changes.
-
----
-
-## 5. Navigation & UX Principles
-- **Outcome-Driven UI**: Visual indicators clearly show the link from execution tasks through deliverables and milestones to KRIs.
-- **Calculated RAG Prompts**: Tooltips and visual cues on the health indicators explain exactly which objective check failed (e.g., *"Amber because task X is marked Blocked"*).
-- **Focused Home**: Overview dashboard defaults to the weekly governance view to keep weekly check-ins highly efficient.
-- **Persistent Header**: Always visible status ensures context is never lost.
-
----
-
-## 6. Out of Scope
-- Direct execution of tasks (task boards are read-only reflections of external tools).
-- Automatic billing or invoice generation (commercials track status but do not process payments).
-- Automated resource allocation scheduling.
-
----
-
-## 7. Expected Outcomes
-- **Total Transparency**: Rayyan and executive leadership can instantly see why any project is red, amber, or green.
-- **No Watermelon Projects**: Objective health logic surfaces hidden delivery risks before they cause delay.
-- **Value Linkage**: Ensures delivery teams focus on achieving KRIs and outcomes rather than just checking off checklist milestones.
-- **Actionable Governance**: Weekly meetings are focused entirely on high-impact items and blockers, reducing meeting overhead.

-# 2. Business Context
-
-## 2.1 Organisation Context
-
-TMaaS is positioned as a DigitalQatalyst (DQ) transformation operating platform designed to industrialise digital transformation delivery through a productised services model. The organisation operates within the digital transformation, advisory, technology delivery, and operational enablement space, serving organisations seeking structured transformation execution rather than isolated consulting engagements.
-
-The platform reflects a shift from project-centric transformation delivery toward reusable operating models, standardised service blueprints, AI-assisted delivery coordination, and governed transformation execution models.
-
-The operating model combines:
-
-- Marketplace-led transformation discovery
-- Structured transformation fulfilment
-- Governance-led operational delivery
-- Cross-functional stakeholder coordination
-- AI-assisted transformation intelligence
-- SaaS-enabled transformation activation
-
-### Business Model Canvas (BMC)
-
-| BMC Domain | Description |
-|---|---|
-| What they do | DQ delivers digital transformation services, operational enablement, governance support, AI-led transformation acceleration, and structured delivery models for organisations undergoing transformation initiatives. |
-| Value Proposition | DQ provides architecture-led transformation delivery that reduces fragmentation, improves governance visibility, standardises delivery execution, and accelerates operational transformation outcomes through reusable service models and AI-assisted coordination. |
-| Customer / Beneficiary Segments | Enterprise organisations, regulated entities, transformation offices, operational leadership teams, programme management stakeholders, transformation sponsors, and organisations requiring structured transformation execution support. |
-| Key Activities | Transformation advisory, service delivery coordination, governance management, delivery oversight, platform operations, blueprint standardisation, operational reporting, AI-assisted transformation guidance, delivery coordination, and transformation fulfilment management. |
-| Key Resources | Transformation methodologies, delivery frameworks, AI-assisted operational intelligence, service blueprints, governance models, delivery teams, operational workflows, transformation data, and digital platform infrastructure. |
-| Key Partners | Technology providers, transformation specialists, cloud vendors, enterprise software vendors, AI service providers, governance stakeholders, and operational delivery partners. |
-| Revenue / Funding | SaaS platform subscriptions, transformation delivery engagements, managed operational services, marketplace-driven service activation, operational support retainers, governance services, and future ecosystem participation models. |
-| Cost Structure | Delivery operations, transformation staffing, platform engineering, AI infrastructure, governance operations, customer onboarding, support operations, cloud infrastructure, and product development investment. |
-| Channels | Direct enterprise sales, transformation consulting engagements, strategic partnerships, operational delivery relationships, digital marketplace experiences, executive engagement channels, and AI-assisted service exploration interfaces. |
-| Customer Relationships | High-touch enterprise relationship management combined with structured operational collaboration, governed delivery engagement, portfolio oversight interactions, and long-running transformation programme coordination. |
-| Regulatory & Compliance Context | TMaaS is designed as a cross-industry platform. Enterprise transformation engagements may involve regulated industries requiring governance controls, auditability, operational transparency, role-based access controls, document traceability, commercial accountability, and data handling controls depending on sector and geography. |
-
-### Transformation Delivery Unit Context
-
-| Unit Context | Detail |
-|---|---|
-| Function & accountability | Owns transformation marketplace operations, delivery governance, delivery coordination, operational oversight, customer engagement enablement, and transformation fulfilment management. |
-| Headcount | Multi-disciplinary transformation team consisting of delivery leadership, operational governance, finance coordination, support operations, product/platform teams, and supplier delivery stakeholders. |
-| Reports to | Executive transformation leadership and strategic delivery oversight functions within DQ. |
-| Governance bodies | Transformation steering groups, operational governance boards, commercial oversight stakeholders, and delivery assurance leadership structures. |
-| Budget authority | Platform operations, transformation delivery operations, delivery coordination, and strategic product investment budgets. |
-| Current systems in use | Fragmented combination of spreadsheets, project tooling, operational trackers, communication platforms, reporting systems, and finance coordination tools. |
-| Key outputs produced | Transformation delivery reporting, governance decisions, operational coordination artefacts, portfolio visibility reports, commercial tracking outputs, fulfilment workflows, and customer delivery updates. |
-
----
-
-## 2.2 Primary Drivers
-
-| Driver | Description | Type | Evidence |
-|---|---|---|---|
-| Fragmented transformation delivery coordination | Enterprise transformation delivery is frequently spread across disconnected project tools, spreadsheets, email chains, governance documents, and operational trackers, creating poor visibility and inconsistent execution governance. | Operational | Common enterprise transformation delivery failure pattern across PMO and transformation environments. |
-| Lack of standardised transformation operating models | Many transformation engagements rely on heavily customised delivery approaches, making scaling difficult and reducing repeatability across engagements. | Operational | Observed across consulting-led and project-led transformation delivery models. |
-| Increasing demand for transformation governance visibility | Executive stakeholders increasingly require operational visibility, measurable delivery oversight, escalation tracking, and portfolio-level reporting across transformation initiatives. | Strategic | Enterprise governance maturity trends and PMO transformation practices. |
-| AI-enabled transformation acceleration expectations | Organisations increasingly expect AI-assisted guidance, operational recommendations, automation support, and intelligent reporting within transformation ecosystems. | Market | Rapid enterprise adoption of AI-assisted operational tooling and decision-support systems. |
-| Difficulty scaling transformation expertise | Transformation delivery expertise is difficult to scale consistently across multiple clients, suppliers, delivery models, and operational environments without structured operating systems. | Operational | Common consulting and transformation scaling constraint. |
-| Need for governed transformation coordination | Multi-party transformation delivery introduces governance, accountability, coordination, and operational consistency challenges across distributed delivery teams. | Operational | Transformation coordination and governance challenge across enterprise delivery programmes. |
-| Transition from project delivery to productised services | Transformation organisations increasingly seek reusable service structures and operational blueprints rather than isolated bespoke delivery engagements. | Strategic | Shift toward platform-enabled professional services and operational productisation models. |
-| Need for operational continuity post-transformation | Organisations increasingly require operational support and managed services after transformation activation rather than one-time delivery engagements. | Market | Growth of managed operational service models following transformation implementation. |
-| Lack of unified transformation intelligence | Delivery data, governance signals, operational metrics, and commercial visibility are often disconnected across systems, reducing strategic decision-making capability. | Operational | Cross-system fragmentation across enterprise transformation operations. |
-| Pressure for auditability and accountability | Transformation programmes increasingly require traceable governance, operational accountability, immutable records, and decision transparency. | Regulatory / Operational | Common governance and regulated-sector operational requirement. |
-
----
-
-# 3. Platform Overview
-
-## 3.1 Platform Purpose
-
-TMaaS is a DQ-owned transformation operating platform and marketplace that enables organisations to discover, activate, govern, deliver, and scale digital transformation initiatives through standardised transformation services, AI-assisted operational intelligence, and structured delivery coordination across internal and external stakeholders.
-
-TMaaS replaces fragmented transformation coordination environments built around spreadsheets, disconnected project tools, static PMO reporting, email-driven governance processes, and isolated operational trackers.
-
-TMaaS complements rather than replaces delivery and operational tooling such as Azure DevOps, Microsoft Planner, spreadsheets, enterprise communication tools, finance coordination systems, and fragmented operational tracking environments. The platform sits above execution systems as the transformation orchestration, fulfilment, governance, operational coordination, and transformation intelligence layer across engagements.
-
-The platform combines capabilities that are usually disconnected across enterprise ecosystems:
-
-- Marketplace discovery
-- SaaS-style service activation
-- Operational transformation fulfilment
-- Governance oversight
-- Portfolio intelligence
-- Commercial coordination
-- AI-assisted transformation operations
-
-No existing single platform combines transformation marketplace discovery with governed downstream operational fulfilment, portfolio governance, AI-assisted delivery intelligence, and commercial alignment within a unified operational architecture.
-
----
-
-## 3.2 Platform Outcomes
-
-| Outcome | Target | Measurement Method | Timing |
-|---|---|---|---|
-| Reduce transformation engagement onboarding time | 60% reduction in onboarding and activation time | Request-to-activation cycle measurement | Leading (30–90 days) |
-| Improve governance visibility across engagements | 90% of active engagements tracked through governed workflows | Governance workflow coverage reporting | Leading (30–90 days) |
-| Reduce operational fragmentation | Replace at least 5 disconnected operational tracking mechanisms per engagement | Tool consolidation and operational workflow audit | Lagging (3–12 months) |
-| Improve executive oversight responsiveness | Reduce escalation response time by 50% | Escalation management SLA reporting | Leading (30–90 days) |
-| Increase reusable transformation delivery adoption | 70% of engagements activated through standardised service blueprints | Blueprint activation analytics | Lagging (3–12 months) |
-
----
-
-## 3.3 Explicit Non-Goals
-
-| Non-Goal | Rationale |
-|---|---|
-| Replace engineering execution tooling | TMaaS governs transformation delivery but does not replace software engineering execution environments. |
-| Replace ERP systems | Financial visibility is in scope, but enterprise accounting and ERP operations remain external. |
-| Operate as an open freelance marketplace | TMaaS is built around governed transformation delivery operations rather than publicly open gig-style participation. |
-| Replace enterprise-wide BI platforms | TMaaS provides transformation-specific operational intelligence rather than enterprise analytics across all business functions. |
-| Provide infrastructure observability tooling | The platform focuses on transformation operations rather than infrastructure telemetry and monitoring. |
-| Provide generic HR lifecycle management | Staffing visibility and operational coordination matter, but HR management is outside platform scope. |
-
----
-
-## 3.4 Working Pattern
-
-| Stage | Activity | Output | Target Date |
-|---|---|---|---|
-| Spec | Define intent, capabilities, segments, features, and requirements | BRS / PRD | |
-| Prototype Shell | Clickable shell with navigation, landing pages, and key flows | Prototype Shell | |
-| Full Prototype | Full workflows and screen interactions without live production data | Functioning Prototype | |
-| Functioning Platform | Production implementation against approved requirements | Production-Ready Platform | |
-| Release | Phased rollout by domain and user segment | Live Platform | |
-
-The TMaaS development lifecycle is not linear. Specification, prototype shell creation, and production engineering may overlap across domains depending on platform maturity, delivery sequencing, and operational dependencies.
-
----
-
-# 4. Platform Specification
-
-## 4.1 Platform Architecture
-
-### DBP Hierarchy Position
-
-| Item | Detail |
-|---|---|
-| Platform ID | DXP.TMaaS |
-| Full Name | TMaaS — Transformation Management as a Service |
-| Hierarchy Level | SoA (Solution of Applications) |
-| Parent Platforms | DXP |
-| Streams | FE |
-| Platform Type | Transformation Marketplace + Transformation Operating System |
-
----
-
-### Stage Model Adoption
-
-```text
-Platform Foundation
-  → IAM
-  → Data model
-  → API gateway
-  → Workflow engine
-  → Notification engine
-  → Analytics event pipeline
-  → Audit infrastructure
-  → AI infrastructure
-  → Design system
-  → CI/CD infrastructure
-
-Stage 0 — Landing & Visitor Onboarding
-Stage 1 — Marketplace Discovery & Enquiry
-Stage 2 — Application & Account Experience
-Stage 3 — Fulfilment, Sales & Operations
-Stage 4 — Transformation Specialisation
-```
-
----
-
-### Stage Activation Model
-
-| Stage | Activated Domains | Unlocked Segments |
-|---|---|---|
-| Foundation | Platform Administration & Governance, AI Infrastructure, Request Infrastructure, Design System Infrastructure, Analytics Infrastructure | All |
-| Stage 0 | Landing Page Experience | Exploring User |
-| Stage 1 | Transformation Marketplace | Exploring User |
-| Stage 2 | Request & Engagement Lifecycle, Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User |
-| Stage 3 | Delivery Operations, Portfolio Oversight & Governance, Commercial & Financial Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Portfolio Oversight User (DQ), Finance User (DQ), Support User (DQ) |
-| Stage 4 | Transformation orchestration intelligence and transformation governance automation | Oversight and Supplier operational users |
-
----
-
-### Domain Structure
-
-| Domain | Purpose |
-|---|---|
-| Landing Page Experience | Marketplace entry and AI-guided discovery |
-| Transformation Marketplace | Service discovery, blueprint catalogue, activation |
-| Request & Engagement Lifecycle | Unified operational request orchestration backbone |
-| Client Delivery Workspace | Client-side engagement visibility and governance participation |
-| Delivery Operations | Supplier-side fulfilment coordination and delivery operations |
-| Portfolio Oversight & Governance | Portfolio intelligence, governance visibility, escalation oversight |
-| Commercial & Financial Operations | Financial visibility and commercial coordination |
-| AI Operational Intelligence | AI-assisted operational intelligence and transformation guidance |
-| Platform Administration & Governance | Governance controls, permissions, workflow administration |
-
----
-
-### Navigation Model
-
-TMaaS uses a role-led navigation architecture.
-
-The platform renders:
-
-- Role-specific landing experiences
-- Role-specific navigation structures
-- Shared operational entities with segmented permissions
-- Contextual intelligence based on operational role
-- Cross-domain operational visibility based on governance access
-
----
-
-### Target Application Architecture
-
-| Tier | Technology | Rule |
-|---|---|---|
-| Client | React / Next.js / React Native | UI rendering only. No business logic in client applications. |
-| Application | Express / BFF | Business logic, orchestration, validation, workflow execution, and access control. |
-| Data | Data API (Supabase / PostgREST / Hasura) + PostgreSQL + Redis | All database access occurs through controlled APIs. No direct raw SQL access from application code. |
-
----
-
-### Universal Request Anchor
-
-`s2_account.requests` acts as the universal operational anchor for all Stage 2+ transactions.
-
-Every:
-- application
-- engagement
-- request
-- operational workflow
-- transformation activation
-- fulfilment process
-- governance review
-- commercial process
-
-must map back to a governed request object.
-
-Stage 4 transformation orchestration capabilities extend the request lifecycle rather than replacing it.
-
----
-
-### Dependency Rule
-
-Dependencies flow downward only.
-
-- Higher stages may reference lower stages.
-- Lower stages may never reference higher stages.
-- Shared operational entities must remain platform-wide.
-- Domain modularity must not fragment shared operational records.
-
----
-
-## 4.2 User Segments

-| Platform activities | Explore services, compare transformation offerings, interact with AI recommendations, submit enquiries, review delivery approaches |

-| Access pattern | Event-triggered |
-| Permissions | Read / submit enquiries |

-## 4.3 Feature Backlog
-
-| Stage | ID | Feature | Domain | Segments | Priority | Notes / Acceptance Criteria |
-|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
-| Foundation | F-PF-01 | Identity & Access Management | Platform Governance | All | P0 | Role-based authentication and workspace rendering must be enforced platform-wide |
-| Foundation | F-PF-02 | Workflow & Approval Engine | Request Lifecycle | All | P0 | Workflow routing and approvals must support configurable operational orchestration |
-| Foundation | F-PF-03 | Notification & Alert Engine | Platform Governance | All | P0 | Notifications must support operational events and governance actions |
-| Foundation | F-PF-04 | AI Infrastructure Layer | AI Intelligence | All | P0 | AI-assisted intelligence must support governance and operational controls |
-| Foundation | F-PF-05 | Audit Trail Infrastructure | Platform Governance | All | P0 | All state changes must generate immutable audit records |
-| Foundation | F-PF-06 | Document Management Infrastructure | Platform Governance | All | P0 | Documents must support governed versioning and role access |
-| Foundation | F-PF-07 | Analytics & Intelligence Pipeline | AI Intelligence | All | P0 | Cross-domain operational intelligence aggregation must exist |
-| Foundation | F-PF-08 | Request Lifecycle Data Model | Request Lifecycle | All | P0 | All operational workflows must anchor to governed request entities |
-| Stage 0 | F-S0-01 | Landing Page Experience | Landing Page Experience | Exploring User | P0 | Visitors must access AI-guided marketplace discovery and marketplace entry experiences |
-| Stage 1 | F-S1-01 | Transformation Marketplace | Marketplace | Exploring User | P0 | Users must browse, filter, and compare transformation services |
-| Stage 1 | F-S1-02 | Service Blueprint Catalogue | Marketplace | Exploring User, Delivery Lead User (DQ) | P0 | Services must be structured into reusable transformation blueprints |
-| Stage 1 | F-S1-03 | AI Service Recommendation Engine | AI Intelligence | Exploring User | P1 | AI must recommend relevant services based on exploration patterns |
-| Stage 1 | F-S1-04 | SaaS Checkout & Activation Flow | Request Lifecycle | Exploring User | P0 | Marketplace users must activate services through governed checkout flows |
-| Stage 1 | F-S1-05 | Marketplace Taxonomy Management | Marketplace | Support User (DQ) | P1 | Marketplace structures and visibility rules must be configurable |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-01 | Client Dashboard | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P0 | Client users must access a consolidated view of engagements, actions, delivery health, and notifications |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-02 | Orders Dashboard | Request & Engagement Lifecycle | Client Delivery Lead User | P0 | Client users must view and track active transformation service orders and engagement statuses |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-03 | Delivery Tracking | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P0 | Client users must track delivery stages, milestones, inputs, and deliverables across engagements |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-04 | Engagement Workspace | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P0 | Client users must access a dedicated workspace for each transformation engagement |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-05 | Chat & Communication | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must communicate directly with DQ delivery teams within engagement workspaces |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-06 | Sessions | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must view, schedule, and manage meetings and working sessions |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-07 | RAID Management | Portfolio Oversight & Governance | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must track risks, issues, assumptions, and dependencies across engagements |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-08 | Documents | Client Delivery Workspace | Client Delivery Lead User | P0 | Client users must access shared files, operational artefacts, and delivery deliverables |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-09 | Notifications & Actions | Platform Administration & Governance | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must view pending approvals, actions, updates, and operational alerts |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-10 | Governance Actions | Portfolio Oversight & Governance | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must review and complete approvals, reviews, and governance-related actions |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-11 | Service Requests | Request & Engagement Lifecycle | Client Delivery Lead User | P1 | Client users must submit and track additional transformation service requests |
-| Stage 2 | F-S2-12 | Profile & Organisation Settings | Platform Administration & Governance | Client Delivery Lead User | P2 | Client users must manage organisation details, users, preferences, and workspace settings |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-01 | Delivery Operations Workspace | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ) | P0 | Supplier operations must coordinate fulfilment workflows and execution |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-02 | Delivery Workflow Queue | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Support User (DQ) | P0 | Operational queues must manage fulfilment dependencies and escalations |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-03 | Portfolio Oversight Dashboard | Oversight | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | P0 | Portfolio visibility must expose operational and governance intelligence |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-04 | Commercial Tracking Workspace | Finance | Finance User (DQ), Delivery Lead User (DQ) | P0 | Commercial workflows must align milestones and invoice states |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-05 | Escalation Management Workspace | Oversight | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Support User (DQ) | P1 | Escalations must support structured routing and resolution tracking |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-06 | Operational Intelligence Dashboard | AI Intelligence | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ), Delivery Lead User (DQ) | P1 | Operational intelligence must identify delivery trends and risks |
-| Stage 3 | F-S3-07 | Support Operations Workspace | Platform Governance | Support User (DQ) | P1 | Support teams must manage onboarding issues and workflow interruptions |
-| Stage 4 | F-S4-01 | Transformation Maturity Intelligence | AI Intelligence | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | P1 | Platform should evaluate transformation maturity across engagements |
-| Stage 4 | F-S4-02 | Blueprint Orchestration Engine | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ) | P1 | Service blueprints should evolve into executable orchestration templates |
-| Stage 4 | F-S4-03 | Transformation Governance Framework Engine | Oversight | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ), Support User (DQ) | P1 | Governance frameworks must support configurable operational models |
-| Stage 4 | F-S4-04 | AI Delivery Optimisation Engine | AI Intelligence | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | P2 | AI should identify fulfilment optimisation opportunities |
-| Stage 4 | F-S4-05 | Managed Operations Service Layer | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Client Delivery Lead User | P2 | Architecture must support post-transformation operational services |
-
----
-
-## 4.4 AI Use Cases
-
-TMaaS uses AI as an embedded operational intelligence layer rather than a standalone assistant feature. AI supports:
-
-- transformation discovery
-- operational guidance
-- delivery intelligence
-- governance visibility
-- workflow recommendations
-- reporting acceleration
-- operational pattern analysis
-
-AI actions remain advisory-only during initial platform releases. Human operational users retain decision authority and governance ownership.
-
-Foundation AI infrastructure includes:
-- LLM API connector
-- Rule engine
-- Event pipeline
-- Human-override workflow
-- AI audit trail
-- AI recommendation governance controls
-
-All AI interactions must support:
-
-- auditability
-- operational traceability
-- role-aware permissions
-- human override controls
-- governed recommendation visibility
-
-### Top 10 AI Use Cases
-
-| Rank | ID | Use Case | Mode | Domains | User Segments | Deploy Timing | Data Dependency | Pain Point Addressed |
-|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
-| 1 | AI-01 | AI Service Recommendation Engine | Generative | Marketplace | Exploring User | Launch | Marketplace interaction data | Difficulty identifying relevant services |
-| 2 | AI-02 | Delivery Risk Detection | Analytical | Delivery Operations, Oversight | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | Launch + 3 months | Workflow and milestone data | Delayed risk visibility |
-| 3 | AI-03 | AI Transformation Guidance Assistant | Generative | Cross-platform | All | Launch | Marketplace and operational interaction data | Operational confusion and fragmented guidance |
-| 4 | AI-04 | Governance Summary Generation | Generative | Oversight | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | Launch + 3 months | Governance reporting data | Manual governance reporting overhead |
-| 5 | AI-05 | Operational Pattern Intelligence | Analytical | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ) | Launch + 6 months | Workflow history | Difficulty identifying fulfilment bottlenecks |
-| 6 | AI-06 | AI Workflow Routing Recommendations | Agentic | Request Lifecycle | Support User (DQ), Delivery Lead User (DQ) | Launch + 6 months | Workflow routing data | Manual coordination inefficiencies |
-| 7 | AI-07 | Transformation Maturity Scoring | Analytical | Oversight | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | Launch + 9 months | Engagement history | Lack of measurable maturity visibility |
-| 8 | AI-08 | Commercial Risk Intelligence | Analytical | Finance | Finance User (DQ) | Launch + 6 months | Invoice and milestone data | Delayed commercial issue detection |
-| 9 | AI-09 | AI Operational Reporting Assistant | Generative | Cross-platform | Delivery Lead User (DQ), Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | Launch + 6 months | Cross-domain operational data | Manual reporting overhead |
-| 10 | AI-10 | Delivery Optimisation Recommendations | Agentic | Delivery Operations | Delivery Lead User (DQ) | Launch + 12 months | Historical fulfilment data | Inefficient operational patterns |
-
----
-
-# 5. Shell Prototype Specification
-
-| Flow ID | Segment | Entry Point | Key Actions | Outcome / Exit State |
-|---|---|---|---|---|
-| FLOW-01 | Exploring User | Marketplace Landing Experience | Explore marketplace → interact with AI recommendations → review blueprint → checkout activation flow → submit request | Governed request created and activation initiated |
-| FLOW-02 | Client Delivery Lead User | Client Dashboard | Review engagement → approve governance action → review milestone → access delivery artefacts | Governance participation recorded and engagement updated |
-| FLOW-03 | Delivery Lead User (DQ) | Supplier Operations Dashboard | Review workflow queue → assign fulfilment tasks → resolve escalation → update operational delivery state | Delivery operations updated and governance traceability maintained |
-| FLOW-04 | Portfolio Oversight User (DQ) | Portfolio Oversight Dashboard | Review transformation metrics → analyse operational risk → access governance summaries → review escalations | Portfolio governance visibility updated |
-| FLOW-05 | Finance User (DQ) | Finance Dashboard | Review invoice lifecycle → align milestone billing → track payment state → resolve commercial issue | Commercial visibility updated and operational alignment maintained |
-| FLOW-06 | Support User (DQ) | Support Operations Dashboard | Review workflow interruption → support onboarding issue → route operational escalation → update support resolution | Operational continuity restored and issue traceability maintained |
-
----
-
-# 6. Design Constraints
-
-| Constraint | Rationale |
-|---|---|
-| Single data model rule | Domain modularity must not fragment shared operational entities across transformation workflows. |
-| Three-tier architecture | Business logic must remain in the application tier to ensure governance consistency and operational control. |
-| Universal request anchor | All Stage 2+ operational workflows must anchor to `s2_account.requests` to maintain lifecycle consistency. |
-| Dependency direction rule | Higher stages may reference lower stages; lower stages must never reference higher stages. |
-| Immutable audit trail | All operational state changes must remain permanently traceable and non-destructible. |
-| ADR requirement | Significant architectural decisions must be documented before implementation begins. |
-| Governance-first permissions | Role-based operational access must enforce transformation governance separation. |
-| AI advisory-first deployment | AI recommendations remain advisory-only during early platform releases. |
-| SaaS-ready marketplace activation | Marketplace architecture must support governed self-service checkout and activation flows. |
-| Cross-industry operational flexibility | Platform structures must support multiple industries without requiring domain fragmentation. |
-| Future orchestration readiness | Service blueprints must evolve toward executable orchestration capability without requiring architectural rebuild. |
-| ERP integration readiness | Commercial operations architecture must support future enterprise integration expansion. |
-
----
-
-# 7. Open Questions
-
-| # | Question | Why Deferred | Owner | Blocking? |
-|---|---|---|---|---|
-| 1 | Should TMaaS support external operational delivery organisations beyond DQ-operated delivery teams? | Ecosystem expansion strategy not yet finalised | Business / Architecture | No |
-| 2 | Should TMaaS support white-labelled deployments for clients or partners? | Commercial deployment model not yet finalised | Business / Product | No |
-| 3 | Should TMaaS be architected as multi-tenant from day one? | Deployment topology not yet finalised | Architecture | No |
-| 4 | Should Portfolio Oversight include cross-client benchmarking intelligence? | Oversight intelligence maturity not yet defined | Product / AI | No |
-| 5 | Should finance operations integrate bi-directionally with ERP systems? | Enterprise integration strategy not yet finalised | Architecture / Finance | No |
-
----
-
-# 8. Appendix
-
-## Comparable Platforms
-
-| Platform | Coverage | Gap Relative to TMaaS |
-|---|---|---|
-| ServiceNow | Workflow orchestration and enterprise operations | Weak transformation marketplace and transformation governance orchestration |
-| Jira Align | Portfolio visibility and agile coordination | Limited transformation governance and operating model support |
-| Salesforce Professional Services Cloud | Professional services coordination | Limited AI-assisted transformation operations |
-| Monday.com Work Management | Workflow collaboration | Weak governance-first transformation structures |
-| Microsoft Dynamics 365 Project Operations | Resource coordination and commercial operations | Limited transformation-specific governance intelligence |
-
----
-
-## Framework References
-
-| Framework | Relevance |
-|---|---|
-| ITIL 4 | Operational workflow governance |
-| TOGAF | Transformation and enterprise architecture governance |
-| COBIT | Governance and operational control |
-| SAFe | Portfolio and delivery coordination |
-| ISO 27001 | Security governance and operational controls |
-
----
-
-## Glossary
-
-| Term | Meaning |
-|---|---|
-| TMaaS | Transformation Management as a Service |
-| DQ | DigitalQatalyst |
-| SoA | Solution of Applications |
-| DXP | Digital Experience Platform stream |
-| DWS | Digital Work Systems stream |
-| Blueprint | Structured transformation service definition and future orchestration template |
-| Request Lifecycle | Unified operational engagement lifecycle anchored to governed request records |
-| Operational Intelligence | AI-assisted governance, delivery, and portfolio insight generation |
-| Governance Action | Operational approval, review, escalation, or governance decision workflow |
-| Fulfilment | Coordinated operational delivery execution across stakeholders |

-## 5. User Journeys
-
-### 5.1 Primary Flow
-1. Unregistered user navigates to `/contact-us`
-2. Views page header/hero
-3. Completes form: Name, Email, Organization, Message
-4. Clicks Submit
-5. Submission creates a record in `s2_account.requests` in CRM frontend
-6. Confirmation toast appears: "Thank you! Your inquiry has been submitted."
-7. User may continue browsing other public pages
-
-### 5.2 Alternate Flows
-- User submits incomplete form → validation errors shown inline
-- Network delay → Loading spinner appears on Submit button
-
-### 5.3 Edge Cases
-- Empty fields trigger inline errors
-- Email format invalid → validation error
-- Message > 1000 characters → truncated or show tooltip
-
-## 6. Fixture Data
-| Entity | ID | Field 1 | Field 2 | Field 3 | Links to |
-|--------|----|---------|---------|---------|---------|
-| ContactFormSubmission | CTS-001 | Name: "Alice Smith" | Email: "alice@example.com" | Message: "Interested in DQ services." | s2_account.requests |
-
-## 7. Shared Components
-- ButtonPrimary: Height 48px, radius 8px, Primary color bg, white text, hover: darker shade, disabled: muted gray
-- InputField: Height 40px, border 1px #D1D5DB, radius 8px, padding 8px
-- TextAreaField: Height 120px, border 1px #D1D5DB, radius 8px, padding 8px
-- ToastNotification: Top-right, auto-dismiss 3s, Background #22C55E, white text
-- HeroSection: Background #F8F9FA, padding 32px, centered heading
-- MapEmbed: 100% width, 320px height, border radius 12px
-
-## 8. Scope
-### In Scope
-- Contact Us page layout and visual design
-- Form submission to inbuilt CRM
-- Validation and success states
-### Out of Scope
-- Registered user variations
-- Multi-language / localization
-- Analytics tracking (planned future)
-- Map interaction beyond display
-
-## 9. Assumptions
-- Only Unregistered users access this page
-- CRM frontend is live and available for lead creation
-- Desktop-first layout; responsive handled separately
-- Form fields limited to Name, Email, Organization, Message
-- Toast appears on successful submission
-

-# Feature: Landing Page — Feature Specific (v1 Corp Mirror)
-
-> **Archive copy.** Canonical active spec: `landing-page-prototype-shell.md` (v1). Product IA evolution: `landing-page-prototype-shell-v2-product-ia.md`.
-
-## 1. Platform Context
-- Platform: DQ Digital Experience Platform (DXP)
-- Type: Generalised
-- Prototype Stage: Feature Specific
-- Stage Coverage: Stage 00
-- Industry: Cross-industry
-- Solution Outcomes:
-  - Validate the primary entry point for unauthenticated Exploring Users.
-  - Communicate the TMaaS value proposition and service model without requiring sign-in.
-  - Drive traffic to the Transformation Marketplace and Contact Us enquiry flow.
-  - Ensure visual alignment with DigitalQatalyst / TMaaS brand and design system.
-
-## 2. Build Approach & References
-- Mode: New Build
-- Reference Builds (Internal): DQ_CORPWEB_PROTOTYPE (mesh backgrounds, corp web tokens)
-- Reference Builds (External): N/A

-## 4. Specification
-
-### 4.1 Brand & Visual System
-- Design System Reference: TMaaS Corp Web Tokens (aligned with DQ_CORPWEB_PROTOTYPE)
-- Colours:
-  - Primary accent: DQ Orange `#FB5535` (hover: `#E04020`)
-  - Primary text / brand: DQ Navy `#030F35`
-  - Background: `#FFFFFF`
-  - Surface alternate: Gray 50 (`oklch(0.98 0.005 264)`)
-  - Text primary: DQ Navy, Text body: Gray 600, Text muted: Gray 400–500
-  - Border: Gray 100–200 (`#E5E7EB` range)
-  - Dark section surfaces: Navy mesh (`--mesh-hero-dark`), CTA mesh (`--mesh-cta-orange`)
-  - Glass / overlay: `white/60`, `white/10`, `white/5` with backdrop blur on secondary actions
-- Typography:
-  - UI font: System sans stack via Tailwind `font-sans`, weights 400–700
-  - Mono font: `font-mono` — used for eyebrow labels, step numbers, metric tags, and mockup UI chrome
-  - Eyebrow pattern: 10–11px, uppercase, wide letter-spacing, accent colour
-  - Page headings: 4xl–8xl responsive scale, semibold, tight tracking
-- Radii: Section cards 16px (`rounded-2xl`), icon containers 12px (`rounded-xl`), buttons pill (`rounded-full`)
-- Spacing: Responsive horizontal padding `px-5` → `md:px-8` → `lg:px-10`; section vertical rhythm `py-12` to `py-24`
-- Effects:
-  - Mesh gradient backgrounds via `MeshSection` variants: `heroLight`, `heroDark`, `ctaOrange`
-  - Optional hero grid overlay on light mesh sections
-  - Orange glow on primary CTAs (`--glow-orange-md`)
-  - Fade-in-up entrance animations on hero content (staggered delays)
-  - Infinite horizontal marquee for trust logos (`marquee-track`, ~35s cycle)
-- Logo: TMaaS logo component, left-aligned in top bar; links to `/`
-
-### 4.2 Layout Shell
-- Viewport target: Desktop-first, responsive from mobile through 1440px+
-- Top bar (Landing Navbar): 64px (`h-16`), sticky, white background, bottom border Gray 100
-  - Left: Logo + primary nav links (desktop `lg+`)
-  - Right: Contact CTA (feature-flagged), mobile menu toggle (`< lg`)
-- Left sidebar: None
-- Main content: Full-width stacked sections, max content width 1200px (`max-w-[1200px]`) or 1280px (`max-w-7xl`) for hero
-- Footer: Full-width, white, top border, four-column grid on desktop collapsing to single column on mobile
-- Scroll anchors: `#offerings`, `#outcomes` (section IDs for in-page / footer deep links)
-
-### 4.3 Personas
-| # | Name | Role | Landing Page | Nav Scope |
-|---|------|------|-------------|-----------|
-| 1 | Exploring User | Unregistered / anonymous visitor | `/` | Public routes only: Landing, Marketplace (if enabled), Contact Us (if enabled) |
-
-### 4.4 Navigation Structure
-- Global (feature-flag controlled)
-  - Logo → `/`
-  - Marketplace → `/marketplace` (flag: `marketplace`)
-  - Contact Us → `/contact` (flag: `contactUs`; navbar CTA + repeated section CTAs)
-- Persona-restricted items: Log in, Get Started, sign-in, dashboard, cart, onboarding, and authenticated workspace routes are not exposed on the landing page in MVP
-- Active state: Current route highlighted in accent colour (e.g. Marketplace nav link when on `/marketplace`, Contact CTA filled state when on `/contact`)
-- Mobile: Hamburger opens full-screen overlay menu below header with the same flag-filtered links
-
-### 4.5 Feature Specification
-- Screens in scope this iteration: `/` (route flag: `homepage`)
-- Demo Storyline: Unauthenticated visitor lands on the homepage, scans value proposition sections, and navigates to the Marketplace or Contact Us to continue their journey
-- Components per screen:
-
-| Screen | Components | Primary Action | States Required |
-|--------|------------|----------------|-----------------|
-| `/` | Landing Navbar | Navigate / open mobile menu | Default / Mobile menu open / Active route highlight |
-| `/` | Hero Section | Primary CTA → Marketplace; Secondary CTA → Contact | Default (animated entrance) |
-| `/` | Hero Dashboard Mockup | N/A (decorative) | Default |
-| `/` | Trusted By Section | N/A | Default (marquee animation) |
-| `/` | Challenge Section | N/A | Default |
-| `/` | Solution Section (3-step model) | Per-step link → Marketplace or Contact | Default / Hover on links |
-| `/` | Offerings Section | Card click → Marketplace | Default / Hover / Focus-visible |
-| `/` | Outcomes Section | N/A | Default / Card hover |
-| `/` | Stats Section | N/A | Default |
-| `/` | Closing CTA Section | Primary CTA → Marketplace; Secondary CTA → Contact | Default |
-| `/` | Footer | External DQ links; internal flag-gated links | Default |
-
-#### 4.5.1 Hero Section
-- Layout: Two-column grid on `lg+` (copy left, mockup right); single column stacked on smaller viewports
-- Content structure (no fixed copy):
-  - Eyebrow label (mono, accent)
-  - Primary headline (two lines; second line in accent colour)
-  - Supporting paragraph (max-width constrained)
-  - Action row: primary pill button with arrow icon; secondary outlined/glass pill button
-- Visual: `MeshSection` variant `heroLight` with grid overlay
-
-#### 4.5.2 Hero Dashboard Mockup
-- Decorative 3D-perspective card simulating a marketplace catalogue UI
-- Contains: sidebar icon rail, header with mono label + title, three mini stat tiles, featured service list (3 items), goal filter chips (3 items)
-- Non-interactive in prototype; no navigation on click
-
-#### 4.5.3 Trusted By Section
-- Eyebrow label centred above marquee
-- Horizontally scrolling logo strip (organisation names as text placeholders; duplicated for seamless loop)
-- Hover: individual logo text darkens to navy
-
-#### 4.5.4 Challenge Section
-- Visual: `MeshSection` variant `heroDark` with subtle grid mask overlay
-- Content structure:
-  - Eyebrow label
-  - Section headline
-  - Supporting paragraph
-  - Three equal-width cards (icon, card title, card body) on `md+`
-  - Closing centred paragraph below cards
-- Card style: glass border (`white/10`), semi-transparent background, icon in accent-tinted container
-
-#### 4.5.5 Solution Section (TMaaS Model)
-- White background section
-- Eyebrow + section headline
-- Three step columns (`sm`: 2-col, `lg`: 3-col), each containing:
-  - Large faded step number
-  - Suite label (mono eyebrow)
-  - Step title
-  - Step description
-  - Text link with arrow → Marketplace (steps 1–2) or Contact (step 3)
-
-#### 4.5.6 Offerings Section
-- Background: Gray 50; `id="offerings"`
-- Eyebrow + section headline
-- Grid of five clickable cards (`md`: 2-col, `lg`: 3-col) — each card: icon, category title, short description, trailing arrow
-- All cards route to `/marketplace`
-- Hover: border accent, shadow lift, icon container inverts to accent fill
-
-#### 4.5.7 Outcomes Section
-- Background: Gray 50; `id="outcomes"`
-- Eyebrow + section headline
-- Three outcome cards (`md`: 3-col), each containing:
-  - Category tag (mono)
-  - Prominent metric value + metric descriptor
-  - Outcome title
-  - Outcome body paragraph
-- Hover: border accent, shadow, metric colour shifts to accent
-
-#### 4.5.8 Stats Section
-- White background
-- Four stat blocks in a row (`md`: 4-col), each with:
-  - Left accent border (4px orange)
-  - Large numeric/value display
-  - Short descriptor label
-
-#### 4.5.9 Closing CTA Section
-- Visual: `MeshSection` variant `ctaOrange` with grid mask overlay; centred content, max-width ~720px
-- Content structure:
-  - Eyebrow label
-  - Headline (inline accent span permitted)
-  - Supporting paragraph
-  - Dual CTAs: primary filled → Marketplace; secondary ghost/outline → Contact
-
-#### 4.5.10 Footer (shared)
-- Brand column: logo + one-line product descriptor
-- Link columns (flag-gated where noted):
-  - External DigitalQatalyst links (always)
-  - Legal links (flag: `legal`)
-  - TMaaS internal links: About (flag: `explore`), AI entry (flag: `chatAssistant`), Marketplace (flag: `marketplace`), Contact (flag: `contactUs`)
-  - Social links: LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube (external, new tab)
-- Bottom bar: copyright year + optional Privacy/Terms (flag: `legal`)
-
-## 5. User Journeys
-
-### 5.1 Primary Flow
-1. Exploring User navigates to `/`
-2. Page renders landing navbar and scrollable section stack
-3. User reads hero value proposition and views dashboard mockup
-4. User scrolls through trust, challenge, solution, offerings, outcomes, and stats sections
-5. User clicks primary CTA (hero or closing section) → navigates to `/marketplace`
-6. User may alternatively click Contact CTA at any exposed point → navigates to `/contact`
-
-### 5.2 Alternate Flows
-- User clicks an offerings card → `/marketplace`
-- User clicks solution step links → `/marketplace` or `/contact` per step
-- User clicks footer Marketplace or Contact link → respective route
-- User opens mobile menu → selects a visible link → menu closes, navigates
-
-### 5.3 Edge Cases
-- `homepage` flag disabled → route redirects to first enabled route per `getFirstEnabledRoute()`
-- `marketplace` flag disabled → Marketplace nav link, hero/CTA primary buttons, offerings cards, solution steps 1–2 links, and footer marketplace link hidden or route-guarded
-- `contactUs` flag disabled → all Contact CTAs hidden
-- Viewport `< lg` → desktop nav hidden; hamburger menu required for navigation
-- Decorative mockup and marquee content are not keyboard-interactive targets
-
-## 6. Fixture Data
-| Entity | ID | Field 1 | Field 2 | Field 3 | Links to |
-|--------|----|---------|---------|---------|----------|
-| TrustLogo | TL-001 | Display name (org placeholder) | — | — | Marquee strip |
-| TrustLogo | TL-002 | Display name (org placeholder) | — | — | Marquee strip |
-| ChallengeCard | CC-001 | Icon: FileX | Card title slot | Card body slot | Challenge section |
-| ChallengeCard | CC-002 | Icon: Layers | Card title slot | Card body slot | Challenge section |
-| ChallengeCard | CC-003 | Icon: Unlink | Card title slot | Card body slot | Challenge section |
-| SolutionStep | SS-001 | Step: 01 / Discover suite | Destination: `/marketplace` | — | Solution section |
-| SolutionStep | SS-002 | Step: 02 / Evaluate suite | Destination: `/marketplace` | — | Solution section |
-| SolutionStep | SS-003 | Step: 03 / Engage suite | Destination: `/contact` | — | Solution section |
-| OfferingCard | OC-001 | Icon: Monitor | Category slot | `/marketplace` | Offerings section |
-| OfferingCard | OC-002 | Icon: Workflow | Category slot | `/marketplace` | Offerings section |

-| OfferingCard | OC-004 | Icon: BarChart2 | Category slot | `/marketplace` | Offerings section |

-| StatBlock | ST-004 | Value slot | Label slot | — | Stats section |
